A1.title: Which managed hosting provider does Acme want to use?

A2.title: Will Acme hold the hosting contract directly, or via EC?

A3.title: Are there any compliance constraints (SOC2, HIPAA, FedRAMP, data residency) that constrain the hosting choice?

B1.title: Can Acme provide a current database export for the migration?

B2.title: Who at Acme owns the migrate/drop editorial decisions, and what cadence works for review?

B3.title: Are there any content areas (gated portals, password-protected sections, internal-only PDFs) that won't show up in a public crawl?

C1.title: For the Salesforce sync — is it one-way (site → SF) or two-way?

C2.title: Should we build the newsletter integration against Mailchimp or HubSpot?

C3.title: Any other third-party systems the site needs to talk to (analytics beyond GA4, marketing automation, CRM, payment, search appliance)?

D1.title: Who manages the DNS cutover, and what's the rollback plan if something breaks at flip time?

D2.title: Is there a content freeze planned, and how will in-flight content from the old site be reconciled on launch day?

A1.title: When does IIE plan to sign the Pantheon contract?

A2.title: While the Pantheon contract is being finalized, can EC begin development on an EC-managed sandbox server, or would IIE prefer to stand up a Pantheon sandbox up-front?

B1.title: Final effort on obtaining a D7 database dump :)

B2.title: How will IIE provide editorial review on the migrate/drop decisions?

B3.title: Beyond the gated content we've already identified, are there other hidden pages, downloads, or asset libraries we should know about?

B4.title: Does IIE agree with the overall migration strategy and risks?

C1.title: What kinds of content do REACs, REAC Assistants, and Sponsors *create* (vs. only review)?

C2.title: When a user logs in via OKTA, what attributes will OKTA send to the new site?

C3.title: Is MFA (multi-factor authentication) enforced on your OKTA tenant?

C4.title: How will Sponsors (State Department staff) authenticate to the new site?

C5.title: How can you provide technical details on OKTA integration?

D1.title: Is there an authoritative list of accredited U.S. higher-education institutions we should validate submissions against?

D2.title: When a non-accredited person submits something, what should the system do — reject silently, reject with a message, route to manual review, or something else?

E1.title: The number and type of maps in the site will depend on the data that needs to be shown in each use case

E2.title: The current site has two parallel country lists — should the new build consolidate?

E3.title: The RFP names several IIE-owned and external resources HEIs should access from the new site — what's the format, refresh cadence, and intended role for each?

E4.title: The map's data layers — which do you want, and what's the source for each?

E5.title: Anything else about the map we should be considering?

F1.title: Scholarship filter vocabularies — confirm existing and fill gaps.

F3.title: Adviser event sub-categorization — what event types within "adviser events" do you want to differentiate?

F5.title: Does IIE agree with the overall approach to Scholarships and Events?

G1.title: Do you have a preferred LLM provider for the AI features?

G2.title: The LLM and Vector DB contracts will be held by IIE directly — can you please confirm?

G3.title: What's your vision for the Greeter / chat assistant?

G4.title: What's your vision for AI-enhanced search?

G5.title: What's your vision for AI-assisted editorial tagging?

G6.title: Does IIE agree with the overall AI feature approach?

H1.title: Which content types should NOT appear in public search results?

I1.title: MailChimp setup for the new site's newsletter signup.

I2.title: Analytics tooling — a few questions to align on.

I3.title: Will the new site require a cookie-consent banner (e.g., for EU visitors under GDPR or California users under CCPA)?

J1.title: Permanent 301 redirects for legacy `educationusa.state.gov` URLs + the State Dept POC who can implement them.

J3.title: SSL certificate — Pantheon-managed Let's Encrypt, or do you need a custom certificate?

J5.title: Outbound email from the site — what SMTP service will we use?

J6.title: Sender domain for outbound email from the site.

K1.title: Does IIE have a GitHub or GitLab organization you'd like the project repository to live in, or should we host it in Electric Citizen's GitHub organization with IIE granted access?

L1.title: Scholarship records — migration scope and View-preservation.

L2.title: Video — how should the new build present the existing video collection?

L3.title: Campus news stories — does this exist as a public-facing content stream on the current site, or only inside the logged-in portal? If public, what URL pattern?

L4.title: Webinars — `/us-higher-education-professionals/online-services/webinars` is linked from the Online Services nav but 301-redirects to the homepage.

L5.title: EducationUSA Academy content — the program ended summer 2024, but its content remains on the live site.

L6.title: Adviser profiles — the LOA references "adviser profiles" as content the new site should support, but the current public-facing site does not surface any.

L9.title: Country Fact Sheets — confirm scope and visibility.

L10.title: Spam protection — the current site uses Drupal image CAPTCHA on one form (`/node/1116`) and nothing on anything else.

L12.title: Region landing pages — what should the new build do with the 12 `/regions/{slug}` pages?

L13.title: Student Success Stories — do you want them on the new site?

M1.title: Campus Hosting submission workflow — currently four external Google Forms power four distinct application paths

M2.title: Event registration is uniformly routed off-site

M3.title: Any other third-party form tools on the current site we haven't surfaced?
